content: List of possible apps here: https://categorytheory.zulipchat.com/integrations/
The first one that springs to mind is Google Calendar, to create a communal calendar for virtual seminars https://categorytheory.zulipchat.com/integrations/doc/google-calendar
No idea how you install these, though
definitely. also we can integrate a zoom room, so that people can meet easily when they want. if anyone has a spare account
So if we'd gone with Discord we'd have easy voice and video chat natively, instead we went with native LaTeX. pros and cons

Jules Hedges said:

List of possible apps here: https://categorytheory.zulipchat.com/integrations/

That's a pretty sick list. Ironically, there's an app for slack so that we can link this community with the (soon-to-be-outdated) ACT slack.
